title: Bilateral agreements and arrangements: International Experience Canada (IEC) – [R204(d) – C21] – Reciprocity – International Mobility Program (IMP) - Canada.ca
content:
## Bilateral agreements and arrangements: International Experience Canada (IEC) – [R204(d) – C21] – Reciprocity – International Mobility Program (IMP)
This section contains policy, procedures and guidance used by IRCC staff. It is posted on the department’s website as a courtesy to stakeholders.
In these instructions “officer” refers to employees of both Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada and the Canada Border Services Agency.
There are 3 possible categories of participation through bilateral agreements and arrangements under the International Experience Canada (IEC) Program:
- Working Holiday (travel and work): Participants are eligible to apply for an open work permit that will allow them to work for any employer at any location in Canada to subsidize their stay as they travel and discover the country.
- Young Professionals (career development): Participants are eligible to apply for an employer-specific work permit that will help them gain professional work experience in Canada.
- International Co-op (Internship) (internships for students only): Participants are eligible to apply for an employer-specific work permit that will help them gain experience in their field of study.
- Discontinuance requirements
- Program requirements specific to each country or territory

## Discontinuance requirements
Where there is a discontinuance requirement (meaning a specific length of discontinuous stay, specified in the agreement), the discontinuous stay will be calculated from the expiry of the first work permit.
For IEC, even though there are discontinuation clauses under the youth mobility agreements (YMAs), the clauses only apply between one IEC participation and the next IEC participation. If a foreign worker has finished their IEC participation and has applied for a work permit renewal in another work permit category, the discontinuation clause in the YMA doesn’t apply. For more information, consult International Experience Canada (IEC) program participants changing work permit categories .
For clients with dual citizenship, discontinuance only applies if the youth is participating consecutively under the same citizenship.
Applications for work permits in the IEC Program are always submitted “outside of Canada” and therefore do not meet the requirements for maintained status for an extension of stay under subsection 183(5) of the Immigration and Refugee Protection Regulations (IRPR) . IEC work permit applications do not meet the requirements for work without a permit under paragraph R186(u) either.

## Program requirements specific to each country or territory
Described below are the program requirements specific to each country or territory that holds a bilateral agreement or arrangement with Canada’s IEC Program:

The student must be registered at a school in Australia at the time of application.
Note: Australian citizens who participated in the program prior to the 2015 quota year are eligible for 1 final participation in the category of their choosing. An applicant is considered to have participated prior to 2015 if their application or applications were submitted to any quota year prior to the launch of the 2015 quota year which took place on March 3, 2015.
There was no participation limit imposed on Australians prior to 2015. As such, Australians may have participated numerous times before the 2015 year. All Australian applicants who participated prior to 2015, regardless of the number of participations are eligible for a final participation of 24 months in the category of their choice.

Applicants are allowed 2 participations under the bilateral agreement and can choose the order of these 2 participations:
- a participation of 24 months in the Working Holiday, Young Professionals or Young Professionals Volontariat International en Entreprise (VIE) category
- a second participation of 12 months in the International Co-op (Internship) category

No specific requirements. Enrolment in a training centre in France is also acceptable
Note 1: French citizens who participated in the program prior to the 2015 quota year are eligible for 1 final participation in the category of their choosing. An applicant is considered to have participated prior to 2015 if their application(s) was submitted to any quota year prior to the launch of the 2015 quota year which took place on February 21, 2015. Those who have participated in IEC more than once before 2015 are no longer eligible.
Note 2: The Government of France established the Volontariat international en Entreprise (V.I.E) program to provide French youth with an opportunity to gain professional experience in an international environment. These youth are assigned to the international branches and subsidiaries of French-based companies. The primary objective of this program is the professional training of high-achieving French youth through international exposure. This allows them to boost their professional development and nurture potential future professional relationships between themselves and the host country.
Business France, the Government of France’s business export development and promotion agency, manages the VIE program. French youth participating in VIE do not receive either a salary or wages but rather a stipend or monthly allowance paid to them directly in France by Business France. This allowance helps VIE participants to cover the cost of living in their host country while also maintaining residential ties to France. VIE participants are to return to France upon completion of their assignment. Business France also guarantees that VIE participants do not become a financial burden for their host country by providing housing and covering any health-care costs.

Note: Before November 1, 2022, residents of Italy could only participate once in the working holiday category, and were issued a 6 month work permit with the possibility to apply for additional 6 months as visitors.
As of November 1;, 2022, Italian citizens may participate in International Experience Canada twice, in any category. Previous participation will count towards the total number of participations allowed. For example, if a candidate submitted an application before November 1, 2022, the terms of the old arrangement apply (they are limited to a 6 month work permit and can apply to extend their stay as a visitor). However, those candidates can now apply for a second (and final) participation of up to 12 months in the category of their choice.
Under the bilateral agreement, applicants are allowed to participate twice .

Note: Inov Contacto is an initiative promoted by His Excellency the Minister of Economy of Portugal and managed by AICEP Portugal Global (the Portuguese Trade & Investment Agency), with the support of the European Social Fund. It is intended for young graduates aged 18 to 29. The program offers nine-month international internships with carefully selected multinational organizations, preferably located in centres of excellence for knowledge and innovation, and in markets of strategic relevance to Portugal. Host countries and employers are selected based on their demonstrated commitment to supporting Inov Contacto trainees. AICEP Portugal Global covers all costs associated with the internship, including logistics, return travel, insurance and living expenses in Canada for the duration of the internship.
Applicants are allowed 2 participations under the bilateral agreement.

### South Korea (Republic of Korea)
Applicants are allowed 2 participations under the bilateral agreement. A repeat category is permitted for the Working Holiday and Young Professionals categories. Applicants can only participate once under the International Co-op (internship) category.
Available categories

International Co-op (internships)
No specific requirement

Note : Before December 11, 2023, citizens of the Republic of Korea only had access to the Working Holiday category and could only participate once in the program for up to 12 months. As of December 11, 2023, citizens of the Republic of Korea may participate in International Experience Canada twice. Previous participation will count toward the total number of participations allowed. For example, if a candidate submitted an application before December 11, 2023, the terms of the old agreement apply (they are limited to a 12-month work permit). However, those candidates can apply for a second (and final) participation of up to 24 months under the Working Holiday, Young Professionals or International Co-op category.
Applicants are allowed 2 participations under the bilateral agreement. A repeat category is not permitted.

Young Professionals ICEX Vives (see note)
3 months discontinuance
Must be a resident of Spain at the time of application
International Co-op (Internship)
3 months discontinuance
Student must be a resident of Spain and must be registered at a school in Spain at the time of application
Note : The Vives program was established by the Government of Spain through ICEX, a federal agency whose mission is the internationalization of the Spanish economy and Spanish companies. Modelled after the French Volontariat international en entreprise (VIE) program, ICEX Vives offers graduate internships to youth with companies legally incorporated in Spain, including subsidiaries, branches and representative offices in Canada. Its primary objective is to provide business development training to Spanish youth.
ICEX manages the Vives program through its offices in Spain. Spanish youth participating in the program do not receive a salary or wages, but they receive a grant paid to them directly by ICEX. The amount is determined according to the city and country of destination, ranging from €21,300 to €42,800 (approximately CAD 31,000 to CAD 62,000 as of December 2022). This allowance helps Vives participants cover round-trip travel expenses as well as living expenses in their host country. Vives participants are also provided with accident and travel insurance for the duration of their internships. Vives participants are to return to Spain upon completion of their placement.

Note: The Taiwan Global Pathfinder Initiative (TGPI) is a youth program for Taiwanese individuals aged 18 to 30 who have either graduated in a specific field of study or gained relevant professional experience. Its purpose is to strengthen international connections, foster innovation and growth across diverse industries, and encourage creative initiatives. The program is managed by the Youth Development Administration (YDA) under the Taiwanese Ministry of Education. Participants are provided with a stipend that covers round-trip airfare, all living expenses and medical insurance. This stipend meets Canadian minimum-wage requirements.
Applicants are allowed 2 participations for a total of 36 months under the bilateral agreement:
- a first participation of 24 months under any category
- a second participation of 12 months under any category

The applicant must be a British citizen from 1 of the following territories: the United Kingdom or the Bailiwick of Jersey and the Bailiwick of Guernsey.
Note: Before December 11, 2023, British citizens only had access to the Working Holiday category and could only participate once in the program for up to 24 months (up to 12 months prior to 2015). As of December 11, 2023, British citizens may participate in International Experience Canada twice in any of the 3 available categories. A previous participation will count toward the total number of participations allowed.
British citizens who participated in the program before 2015 for up to 12 months are eligible for 1 final participation of 24 months in any category. Applicants are considered to have participated if they arrived in Canada and their work permit was issued.
Those who already participated in IEC twice are no longer eligible.
